decisions.summary: Fix the bug WITHIN the PAN-1737 generations-chain architecture; do NOT revert to a single force-reset branch per day. The issue's literal 'Suggested fix #1' is declined because it would tear out the deliberately shipped multi-generation chain + per-generation live stacks. The issue quotes the OLD PAN-1691 design intent, apparently unaware PAN-1737 replaced it on purpose.

Two independent root-cause defects, two beads. Defect 1 (proliferation): roles/flywheel.md tells the orchestrator to POST /api/flywheel/assemble-uat every tick, but that endpoint is now a FORCED reconcile (runUatTrainReconcile({force:true})) that bypasses the liveSignatureMatches idempotency guard and assembles a new generation every cycle. Defect 2 (non-determinism): makeUatCandidateName defaults its start index to Math.random, so even legitimate new generations get unordered names.

Codename determinism is a ONE-LINE change: default makeUatCandidateName's pick to () => 0 instead of Math.random. The existing collision-walk then assigns otter -> falcon -> cedar in generation order. No generations-store changes; the append-only chain is untouched. (Considered + rejected: bundle-content-hash naming, because it would require name-reuse / in-place row mutation that breaks the append-only chain.)

Surface the active candidate via a revived dedicated read endpoint GET /api/flywheel/uat-candidate (newest 'ready' generation: branchName + bundled issue IDs + status), NOT by adding a field to the shared FlywheelStatus Effect-Schema contract. The issue accepts either ('GET /api/flywheel/current ... or a uat-candidate read endpoint'); the dedicated endpoint has the smaller blast radius and revives the endpoint roles/flywheel.md already references but which PAN-1737 removed.

No reaper code change. buildUatGenerationCleanupGit().deleteBranch already runs 'git push origin --delete' + local delete with keep=3; the 4-branch pile-up is keep=3 outpaced by per-tick proliferation, which self-corrects once Defect 1 is fixed. Verify-not-fix (investigate-before-fixing).

hazards.summary: ARCHITECTURAL FORK (loud): the issue's Suggested fix #1 ('make the codename deterministic per day so each assembly force-resets the SAME uat/<label>-<MMDD> branch') would collapse the PAN-1737 generations chain to one branch/day. This plan deliberately does NOT do that — generations legitimately differ when the bundle changes (e.g. RUN-35's cobalt bundled PAN-1242 that birch/willow did not). A work agent must NOT read the issue literally and rip out the generations chain.
hazards.mitigation: NonGoals in the vBRIEF explicitly forbid the revert (review enforces NonGoals as must-not constraints). The fix targets redundant same-bundle proliferation (Bead A) + unordered names (Bead B), not legitimate generation turnover. If the operator actually wants single-branch-per-day, that is a separate decision to escalate, not this bug.

hazards.summary: Regression risk: removing the every-tick forced assemble could stop UAT assembly entirely if the internal reconciler did not cover the require_uat case.
hazards.mitigation: Verified during planning: startUatTrainReconciler() is wired unconditionally in main.ts:400; the internal 60s reconciler auto-assembles whenever isMergeTrainEnabled() + active run, independent of require_uat. So assembly continues without the forced call. When merge_train is off, even the forced endpoint no-ops via isEnabled() — no edge regression.

hazards.summary: A RUNNING flywheel will not pick up roles/flywheel.md changes (Bead A) until a new run / restart. The fix won't take effect on the live RUN until then.
hazards.mitigation: Operational, not a code concern. Note it in the PR/handoff so the operator restarts the flywheel run after merge to apply the role-doc fix.

hazards.summary: Bead A is the only behavioral proliferation fix but is a role-PROMPT edit, so it cannot be locked by a unit test; the work agent could misread 'stop forcing' and remove UAT assembly altogether.
hazards.mitigation: The PRD gives exact before/after role-doc text so the diff is mechanically verifiable. Bead A's acceptance criteria assert the doc no longer contains an unconditional per-tick forced-assemble instruction and references the live endpoints.

sessionHistory.note: Root-caused two independent defects (stale force-every-tick role doc + random codename); chose fix-within-generations over the issue's literal single-branch revert. 3 beads (role-doc fix, deterministic codename, uat-candidate read endpoint). Advisor-reviewed.
